articles.title: Over 50 families without electricity for 8 days amid severe heatwave in Nabarangpur - Odisha TV
articles.full_text:
As Odisha continues to reel under intense heatwave conditions, more than 50 families in a remote village of Nabarangpur district have been forced to live without electricity for the past eight days. The prolonged power outage has triggered severe hardship for residents already battling scorching temperatures and unbearable humidity, sources said on Friday.
The incident has been reported from Jani Sahi in Bhalukanadi village under Jharigaon block of the district.
According to villagers, electricity supply to the area was completely disrupted after the village transformer developed a technical fault. Since then, residents have allegedly been running from pillar to post requesting authorities to restore power supply, but no concrete action was taken for several days.
The power crisis comes at a time when daytime temperatures across the State have crossed 42 degrees Celsius in many areas. Nights have also become extremely uncomfortable due to high humidity, making life miserable for villagers living without fans, lights, or other basic electrical facilities.
Residents alleged that despite repeated visits to the Jharigaon electricity office, neither officials nor staff paid attention to their grievances. Villagers said children are unable to study at night due to a lack of lighting, while elderly people and small children are facing health problems because of the severe heat and sleepless nights.
The absence of electricity has also raised safety concerns in the village. Locals fear snakes and other poisonous insects may enter houses during the night due to the complete darkness in the area.
The situation has sparked anger among villagers, especially as the State government had earlier directed that electricity supply should not be disconnected during the ongoing heatwave, even in cases of pending electricity bills. Residents alleged that the electricity department’s negligence has completely ignored these instructions.
Following media attention and public outrage, the department’s SDO reportedly assured over the phone that the damaged transformer would be replaced and the electricity supply restored soon.
“They (the villagers) have been saying for eight days now, but I haven't received any such information yet. It has been 3-4 days. They had made a complaint on the 25th or 26th. Because of the Kalbaisakhi storm, the transformer got damaged. They are also holding back a lot of pending money (due bill amounts). I told them (the locals) today to replace the transformer,” Umerkote electrical department SDO, Swarup Patra, said when contacted.
Reported By: Trinath Dharua
